evals.prompt: Create a new skill called 'docker-helper' for managing Docker containers
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the skill directory with SKILL.md containing proper frontmatter (name, description with trigger keywords), step-by-step instructions, and references to detailed docs. Creates evals/evals.json with test cases.
assertions: The agent creates a SKILL.md with valid frontmatter (name and description fields)
The description includes 'Use when...' trigger phrases
The SKILL.md body is under 250 lines
The agent creates evals/evals.json with at least 2 test cases

evals.prompt: The description for my 'git-flow' skill isn't triggering correctly. It only fires 30% of the time when users ask about git workflows.
evals.expected_output: The agent analyzes the description, suggests making it more imperative and specific, adding trigger keywords, and testing with eval queries. May suggest broadening scope or adding context.
assertions: The agent analyzes the current description for trigger accuracy
The agent suggests specific improvements to the description
The agent mentions testing with eval queries

evals.prompt: Write eval test cases for my existing skill
evals.expected_output: The agent creates evals/evals.json with realistic prompts, expected outputs, and checkable assertions. Includes variety: formal/casual, terse/context-heavy, near-miss queries.
assertions: The agent creates evals with id, prompt, expected_output, and assertions fields
The prompts are realistic and varied
The assertions are concrete and checkable (not subjective)

evals.prompt: What fields are required in the SKILL.md frontmatter?
evals.expected_output: The agent lists: name (required, max 64 chars, lowercase/hyphens) and description (required, max 1024 chars). Optional: license, compatibility, metadata, allowed-tools.
assertions: The agent lists name as required with constraints
The agent lists description as required with constraints
The agent mentions the 250-line SKILL.md body limit

evals.prompt: Review my skill's References section - it uses @references/filename.md and markdown links [text](reference/file.md). Is this correct?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the incorrect formats and explains the correct format: use backticks without @ prefix (e.g., `reference/filename.md` - Description). No markdown links, no @ prefix.
assertions: The agent identifies @references/ as incorrect format
The agent identifies markdown links as incorrect format
The agent explains correct format is backtick-wrapped reference path with description

evals.prompt: I want to create a skill for EPUB validation. What should I include in the description to make it trigger reliably?
evals.expected_output: The agent suggests using imperative phrasing ('Use this skill when...'), focusing on user intent (validating EPUBs), listing specific trigger scenarios (EPUB upload, validation errors, format checks), and keeping it concise.
assertions: The agent suggests imperative phrasing ('Use when...')
The agent mentions listing trigger scenarios
The agent mentions keeping description under 1024 chars
